standards.title: Tables use snake_case plural nouns
standards.description: Table names must be lowercase snake_case and plural. No prefixes such as tbl_ or t_. No camelCase or PascalCase.
standards.rationale: PostgreSQL silently lowercases unquoted identifiers, making camelCase invisible at the SQL layer and requiring double-quoting everywhere. Supabase auto-generates TypeScript types and API routes directly from table names — inconsistent casing produces inconsistent API surfaces.
standards.agent_guidance: Before generating any CREATE TABLE statement, verify the name is lowercase snake_case and plural. Reject names that are singular, camelCase, PascalCase, or prefixed. For junction tables, combine both table names alphabetically: course_users not user_courses.

standards.title: All user-initiated write actions must produce an activity log entry
standards.description: Every create, update, delete, or status-change action initiated by an authenticated user must write a corresponding entry to the activity log capturing who, what, which entity, and when.
standards.rationale: Activity logs are the foundation of auditability and debugging. Without them there is no way to answer who changed this and when. Retroactive logging is not possible — entries must be written at the time of the action.

valid: User creates a workflow → activity_log: actor_id, action='workflow.created', entity_type, entity_id, occurred_at
Admin deletes a member → activity_log: actor_id, action='member.deleted', entity_type, entity_id, occurred_at
User updates a profile field → activity_log: actor_id, action='profile.updated', entity_type, entity_id, changes={field, old, new}, occurred_at
invalid: Workflow deleted with no activity log entry written
Log entry written without actor_id — attributed to system instead of user
Log entry written after the transaction commits via a background job with no synchronous fallback

acceptance_criteria: Given an authenticated user, when they create any entity, then an activity_log entry is written within the same transaction with actor_id, action='{entity}.created', entity_type, entity_id, and occurred_at
Given an authenticated user, when they update an entity, then an activity_log entry is written with action='{entity}.updated' and a changes object recording field, old value, and new value
Given an authenticated user, when they delete or archive an entity, then an activity_log entry is written before or within the same transaction as the deletion
Given any write action, when the activity log write fails, then the entire transaction is rolled back and the action does not succeed
standards.agent_guidance: Whenever generating a backend mutation — insert, update, delete, or status change — also generate an insert into activity_log within the same transaction. Required fields: actor_id, action (format: '{entity_type}.{verb}'), entity_type, entity_id, occurred_at (default now()). For updates include a changes jsonb column with before/after values. Never write the log entry in a separate job or after the transaction commits.

exceptions.scope: bulk-import feature only — single completion entry written per job, not per row
